summary: Full e2e testing of Hyper-Local Weather Map (backend + frontend). 10/10 backend pytest cases pass; all frontend flows (map click, search, save/remove, layer toggle, units toggle) working. OWM API key confirmed never leaks to browser (HTML/source/network).

issues: When the right-side SAVED LOCATIONS slide-panel is open it visually overlaps the WeatherCard on the right edge. Not blocking interaction (weather-units-toggle still accessible after closing) but the card content is partially hidden. Consider hiding the card or pushing it left when saved panel is open.

critical_code_review_comments: server.py: bare `os.environ['OWM_API_KEY']` will crash the app at import if the key isn't set. Fine for prod but consider a clearer error message.
server.py: /api/forecast and /api/weather share `weather_cache`. Keys are different (w: vs f:) so no collision, but using the same TTL for both is fine; just documenting.

server.py: HTTPException from upstream may propagate non-standard status codes (e.g., 401 from OWM exposed downstream). Consider mapping >=500 upstream errors to 503 to avoid leaking provider semantics.
Tile proxy caches binary content in memory (maxsize=2000). At ~10-50KB/tile that's up to 100MB; acceptable for v1.
Frontend uses placeholder 'SEARCH ANY VILLAGE, TOWN OR CITY…' and dark Command Center theme renders correctly with adequate contrast.
seed_data_creation: None - app has no persistence layer; saved locations only in localStorage (hlw:saved_locations).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend regression suite at /app/backend/tests/test_weather_api.py covers /api/, /api/weather (incl. 422 validation + cache), /api/forecast, /api/geocode/reverse, /api/geocode/search, /api/tiles/{layer}/{z}/{x}/{y}.png (valid+invalid), and verifies API key never leaks. Frontend testids confirmed: search-input, search-result-N, weather-card, weather-location-name, weather-temp, metric-wind, metric-humidity, weather-save-btn, open-saved-btn, saved-item-*, saved-remove-*, layer-toggle-{clouds,precipitation,temp,wind}, weather-units-toggle. To click map use page.mouse.click on .leaflet-container bounding box center. Note: weather-units-toggle is rendered inside WeatherCard so the card must be visible (close saved panel first).
rca of the issue: No bugs found. Initial test run reported weather-units-toggle as MISSING; root cause was that the testid is rendered inside WeatherCard which was overlapped by the open Saved Locations panel during the assertion. Re-running with the panel closed showed the toggle works correctly (°C 12° → °F 54° for Paris).
